enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The Authorised Persons may refer to respective circulars/ notifications for detailed information, if so needed.
enriched_requirements.object: notifications for detailed information

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: It may be noted that whenever necessary, Reserve Bank shall issue directions to Authorised Persons through A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: Persons through A

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The Master Direction issued herewith shall be amended suitably simultaneously.

This document contains the various instructions relating to the subject of money
changing activities including functioning of Full Fledged Money Changers (FFMCs),
non-bank Authorised Dealers Category-II as well as the conduct of foreign exchange
transactions with their customers/constituents.3
3 Para 1 of Section I modified on May 06, 2026.
No person shall carry on money changing business without the possession of a valid money changer's licence issued by the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: business without the possession of a valid money changer's licence issued by the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Definitions

2.1 Authorised Person means an authorised dealer, money changer, off-shore banking unit or any other person authorised under sub-section (1) of section 10 to deal in foreign exchange or foreign securities.
enriched_requirements.object: changer, off-shore banking unit or any other person authorised under sub-section (1) of section 10 to deal in foreign exch

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 2.2 'Authorised Dealer' (AD) means a person authorised as an authorised dealer under sub-section (1) of section 10 of FEMA.
enriched_requirements.object: an authorised dealer under sub-section (1) of section 10 of FEMA

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
2.3 'Authorised Dealer (AD) Category I' means entities which are authorised by the Reserve Bank to carry out all permissible current and capital account transactions as per directions issued from time- to-time

2.4 'Authorised Dealer (AD) Category II' means entities which are authorised by the Reserve Bank to carry out specified non-trade related current account transactions, all the activities permitted to Full Fledged Money Changers and any other activity as decided by the Reserve Bank, and shall include (i) Upgraded FFMCs;
2.5 'Authorised Dealer (AD) Category III' means entities which are authorised by the Reserve Bank to carry out specific foreign exchange transactions incidental to their business / activities.
enriched_requirements.object: by the Reserve Bank to carry out specific foreign exchange transactions incidental to their business / activities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Note: Authorised Dealer Category-I Banks/ Authorised Dealers Category-II/ FFMCs may appoint franchisees, at their option, for the purpose of carrying restricted money changing business i.e.
enriched_requirements.object: option, for the purpose of carrying restricted money changing business i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The minimum Net Owned Funds (NOF) required for consideration as FFMC are as follows:
Category Minimum NOF Single branch FFMC Rs.25 lakh Multiple branch FFMC Rs.50 lakh Note: The Net Owned Funds of applicants, other than banks, should be calculated as per the following.
enriched_requirements.object: the following

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Documentation Application in the prescribed form should be submitted through the APConnect application (https://apconnect.rbi.org.in/entity) to the respective Regional Office of the Foreign Exchange Department of the Reserve Bank under whose jurisdiction the registered office of the applicant falls, along with the following documents:

(a) Copy of the Certificate of Incorporation.
enriched_requirements.object: APConnect application (https://apconnect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (f) On obtaining approval from the Reserve Bank, a copy of the registration under Shops & Establishment Act or any other documentary evidence such as rent receipt, copy of lease agreement, etc., should be submitted to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank before commencement of the business.
enriched_requirements.object: Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank before commencement of the business

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (g) The FFMC should commence its operations within a period of six months from the date of issuance of licence and inform the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: a period of six months from the date of issuance of licence and inform the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Ban

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (h) The new FFMCs should carry out their activities as per the instructions specified in SECTIONS V and VI below and other instructions issued by the Reserve Bank from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: as per the instructions specified in SECTIONS V and VI below and other instructions issued by the Reserve Bank from time to

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: No FFMC/non-bank AD Category II shall carry on money changing business at any additional place of business other than its permanent place of business except with the prior approval of the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: business at any additional place of business other than its permanent place of business except with the prior approval of the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: An FFMC/non-bank AD Category II which intends to commence money changing business at any additional place of business shall apply 3through the APConnect application to the respective Regional Office of the Foreign Exchange Department under whose jurisdiction the registered office of the applicant falls and the Reserve Bank may approve the additional place of business subject to such conditions as deem fit.
enriched_requirements.object: application to the respective Regional Office of the Foreign Exchange Department under whose jurisdiction the registered

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: It is expected that branches of Authorised Persons should be diversified and should be meeting the demand of tourists, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: be meeting the demand of tourists, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Applications for additional locations (places of business) should be accompanied by the following:-

(a) A certificate from Proprietor/Partner/Director /CFO of the entity as regards the position of NOF.
enriched_requirements.object: following:- (a) A certificate from Proprietor/Partner/Director /CFO of the entity as regards the position of NOF

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Note: No new branch licence shall be issued to any FFMC/non-bank AD Category-II, against whom any major DoE / DRI case is pending.
enriched_requirements.object: FFMC/non-bank AD Category-II, against whom any major DoE / DRI case is pending

conditions: in case there is a change
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, in case there is a change, a copy of the revised / latest version of the policy shall be required to be submitted.
enriched_requirements.object: submitted

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: A copy of the registration under Shops & Establishment Act or any other documentary evidence such as rent receipt, copy of lease agreement, etc., should be submitted to the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank before commencement of business at an additional branch.
enriched_requirements.object: Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank before commencement of business at an additional branch

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The FFMC/ non-bank AD Category II should commence operations of its additional branch within a period of six months from the date of issuance of licence and inform the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: additional branch within a period of six months from the date of issuance of licence and inform the Regional Office concern

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: For opening Foreign Exchange Counters/branches/) at the international airports in India, AD Category-I banks/ ADs Category - II/ FFMCs should adhere to following conditions.
(a) Foreign Exchange Counters/branches in the arrival halls in international airports in India shall ideally be established after the Customs Desk (Green Channel/Red Channel).
enriched_requirements.object: the Customs Desk (Green Channel/Red Channel)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, Foreign Exchange Counters may also be established between the Immigration Desk and the Customs Desk in international airports in India subject to the condition that these counters shall only purchase foreign currency and sell Indian Rupees (INR) and "Encashment Certificates" shall invariable be issued by the money changers to the customers.
enriched_requirements.object: and sell Indian Rupees (INR) and "Encashment Certificates" shall invariable be issued by the money changers to the cust

enriched_requirements.timeline:
within 10 kms from the borders of Pakistan and Bangladesh may also
sell the currency of the bordering country
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, franchisees of AD Category - I / AD Category - II / FFMCs
functioning within 10 kms from the borders of Pakistan and Bangladesh may also
sell the currency of the bordering country, with the prior approval of the Regional
Office concerned of the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: of the bordering country, with the prior approval of the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding anything
contained in these directions, an authorised person shall not enter into any fresh
franchisee arrangements henceforth, and the existing franchisee arrangements shall
be discontinued gradually, in any case within two years from May 6, 2026.7
Note: Obtaining of Conduct Certificate of the franchisee from the local police authorities is optional for the franchisers.
enriched_requirements.object: franchisee arrangements henceforth, and the existing franchisee arrangements shall be discontinued gradually, in any case within

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, the franchisers may take due care to avoid appointing individuals/ entities as franchisees who have cases / proceedings initiated / pending against them by any law enforcing agencies.
enriched_requirements.object: avoid appointing individuals/ entities as franchisees who have cases / proceedings initiated / pending against them by any law enforc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The above checks should be done on a regular basis, at least once in a year.
enriched_requirements.object: regular basis, at least once in a year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
franchiser should obtain from the franchisees proper documentary evidence
confirming the location of the franchisees in addition to personal visits to the site.
enriched_requirements.object: proper documentary evidence confirming the location of the franchisees in addition to personal visits to the site

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The franchiser should also obtain a Chartered Accountant's certificate confirming the
new franchisee agreements are entered into, these would have to be reported (through the APConnect application by non-bank AD Category-II and FFMCs) to the Reserve Bank in Form RMC- F on a post-facto basis along with similar declaration as indicated above."
maintenance of minimum Net Owned Funds of the franchisee, i.e., Rs.10 lakh on an
ongoing basis.
enriched_requirements.object: Accountant's certificate confirming the new franchisee agreements are entered into, these would have to be reported (through t

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (b) Foreign Exchange Counters/branches in the departure halls in international airports in India shall be established before the Customs Desk or the immigration desk, whichever comes first.
enriched_requirements.object: Customs Desk or the immigration desk, whichever comes first

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, the Foreign Exchange Counters established in the Duty-Free Area or Security Hold Area beyond the Immigration or Customs desk shall allow residents (along with non-residents) to exchange Indian Rupee notes.
enriched_requirements.object: non-residents) to exchange Indian Rupee notes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The limits of carrying Indian Rupees by residents and non-residents shall be as per the para given below at (c) and (d).
enriched_requirements.object: para given below at (c) and (d)

enriched_requirements.timeline: within 10 kms from the borders of Pakistan and Bangladesh may also sell the currency of the bordering country
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: However, franchisees of AD Category - I / AD Category - II / FFMCs functioning within 10 kms from the borders of Pakistan and Bangladesh may also sell the currency of the bordering country, with the prior approval of the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: of the bordering country, with the prior approval of the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Agency / Franchisee agreement to be entered into should include the following
salient features:
enriched_requirements.object: features:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) The franchisees should display the names of their franchisers, exchange
rates and that they are authoried only to purchase foreign currency,
prominently in their offices.
enriched_requirements.object: their franchisers, exchange rates and that they are authoried only to purchase foreign currency, prominently in their offices

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Exchange Rate for conversion of foreign currency
into Rupees should be the same or close to the daily exchange rate charged
by the AD Category - I Banks / ADs Category - II / FFMC at its branches.
enriched_requirements.object: close to the daily exchange rate charged by the AD Category - I Banks / ADs Category - II / FFMC at its branches

enriched_requirements.timeline: within 7 working days from the date of purchase
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The foreign currency purchased by the franchisee should be surrendered only
to its franchiser within 7 working days from the date of purchase.
enriched_requirements.object: its franchiser within 7 working days from the date of purchase

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) The on-site inspection of the franchisee by the franchiser should be
conducted at least once a year.
enriched_requirements.object: once a year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Procedure for application

An AD Category - I Bank / AD Category - II/ FFMC should apply 5(non-bank AD Category -II and FFMCs should apply through the APConnect application) to the respective Regional Office of the Reserve Bank, in Form RMC-F for appointment of franchisees under this Scheme.
enriched_requirements.object: -II and FFMCs should apply through the APConnect application) to the respective Regional Office of the Reserve Bank, in F

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The application should be accompanied by a declaration that while selecting the franchisees, adequate due diligence has been carried out and that such entities have undertaken to comply with all the provisions of the franchising agreement and prevailing Reserve Bank regulations regarding money changing.
enriched_requirements.object: declaration that while selecting the franchisees, adequate due diligence has been carried out and that such entities have undertak

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AD Category-I Bank/ AD Category-II / FFMC should undertake the
following minimum checks while conducting due diligence of its franchisees:
i.
enriched_requirements.object: checks while conducting due diligence of its franchisees: i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Encashment Certificate
Note: Instructions in the Master Direction - Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) dated January 1, 2016 (updated from time to time), as applicable, shall apply.

conditions:
when asked for in
cases of purchases of foreign currency notes
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Authorised Persons may issue certificate of encashment when asked for in
cases of purchases of foreign currency notes, coins and travellers cheques from
residents as well as non-residents.
enriched_requirements.object: when asked for in cases of purchases of foreign currency notes, coins and travellers cheques from residents as well as

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
These certificates bearing authorised signatures
should be issued on the letter head of the money changer and proper record should
be maintained.
enriched_requirements.object: letter head of the money changer and proper record should be maintained

conditions: where encashment certificate is not issued
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) In cases where encashment certificate is not issued, attention of the
customers should be drawn to the fact that unspent local currency held by non-
residents will be allowed to be converted into foreign currency only against
production of a valid encashment certificate.
enriched_requirements.object: fact that unspent local currency held by non- residents will be allowed to be converted into foreign currency only against production

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Purchases from other FFMCs and Authorised Dealers (ADs)
An FFMC/ non-bank AD Category II may purchase from other FFMCs and
Authorised Dealers (ADs) any foreign currency notes, coins and encashed traveller's
cheques tendered in the normal course of business.
enriched_requirements.object: and Authorised Dealers (ADs) any foreign currency notes, coins and encashed traveller's cheques tendered in the normal cours

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Rupee equivalent of the amount
of foreign exchange purchased should be paid only by way of crossed account
payee cheque/demand draft/banker's cheque / pay order/electronic funds transfer
through banking channel.
enriched_requirements.object: way of crossed account payee cheque/demand draft/banker's cheque / pay order/electronic funds transfer through banking channel

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(I) Private Visits
Authorised Persons may sell foreign exchange up to the prescribed ceiling specified
in Schedule III to the Foreign Exchange Management (Current Account Transaction)
Rules, 2000 during a financial year to persons resident in India for undertaking one
or more private visits to any country abroad (except Nepal and Bhutan).
enriched_requirements.object: to the prescribed ceiling specified in Schedule III to the Foreign Exchange Management (Current Account Transaction) Rules, 2

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(II) Business visits
Authorised Persons may sell foreign exchange to persons resident in India for
undertaking business travel or for attending a conference or specialised training or
for maintenance expenses of a patient going abroad for medical treatment or check -
up abroad or for accompanying as attendant to a patient going abroad for medical
treatment / check-up up to the limits specified in Schedule III to FEMA (Current
Account Transactions) Rules, 2000.
enriched_requirements.object: persons resident in India for undertaking business travel or for attending a conference or specialised training or for mainte

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(III) Forex Pre-paid Cards
Authorised Dealers Category-II may issue forex pre-paid cards to residents travelling
on private/business visit abroad, subject to KYC/AML/CFT requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: to residents travelling on private/business visit abroad

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However,
the settlement in respect of forex pre-paid cards shall be effected through AD
Category-I banks only.13
In this regard, it is clarified that prepaid foreign currency cards are a form of foreign
currency, similar to foreign currency notes or travellers cheques.
enriched_requirements.object: Category-I banks only

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
As such, the
authorised dealers (ADs)/FFMCs selling pre-paid foreign currency cards for travel
purposes are required to comply with the same rigorous standards of due diligence
and KYC as they would in case they were selling foreign currency notes/ travellers
cheques to their customers.
enriched_requirements.object: same rigorous standards of due diligence and KYC as they would in case they were selling foreign currency notes/ travellers cheques

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 13 Para 5(III) of Section V modified on May 06, 2026.

Prior to modification, it read as follows:

"Authorised Dealers Category-II may issue forex pre-paid cards to residents travelling on private/business visit abroad, subject to KYC/AML/CFT requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: to residents travelling on private/business visit abroad

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, the settlement in respect of forex pre-paid cards may be effected through AD Category-I banks."
Conditions
i.
enriched_requirements.object: Category-I banks

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Reserve Bank will not generally, prescribe the documents which should be
verified by the Authorised Persons while releasing foreign exchange.
enriched_requirements.object: Authorised Persons while releasing foreign exchange

conditions: In case of issue of travellers' cheques
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In case of issue of travellers' cheques, the traveler should sign the cheques in
the presence of an authorised official and the purchaser's acknowledgement for
receipt of the travellers' cheques should be held on record.
enriched_requirements.object: the presence of an authorised official and the purchaser's acknowledgement for receipt of the travellers' cheques should be held o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Persons may release foreign exchange for travel purposes on the
basis of a declaration given by the traveler regarding the amount of foreign
exchange availed of during the financial year.
enriched_requirements.object: travel purposes on the basis of a declaration given by the traveler regarding the amount of foreign exchange availed of d

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Authorised Persons may accept payment in cash below Rs.50,000/- (Rupees fifty
thousand only) against sale of foreign exchange for travel abroad (for private visit
or for any other purpose).
enriched_requirements.object: below Rs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever the sale of foreign exchange is for the
amount equivalent to Rs.50,000/- and above whether it involves a single drawal
or multiple drawals for a single journey, the payment must be received only by a
crossed cheque drawn on the applicant's bank account or crossed cheque drawn
on the bank account of the firm sponsoring the visit of the applicant or electronic
funds transfer through banking channel by applicant or sponsoring company or
banker's cheque / pay order / demand draft.
enriched_requirements.object: a crossed cheque drawn on the applicant's bank account or crossed cheque drawn on the bank account of the firm sponsoring the visi

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the payment by
Rupees/ through crossed cheque/ banker's cheque/ pay order/ demand
draft/electronic funds transfer through banking channel, Authorised Persons may
also accept the payments made by the traveller through debit cards/ credit cards/
prepaid cards for travel abroad (for private visit or for any other purpose)
provided- (i) KYC/ AML / CFT guidelines are complied with, (ii) sale of foreign
currency/ issue of foreign currency traveller's cheques is within the limits (credit/
prepaid cards) prescribed by the bank, (iii) the purchaser of foreign currency/
foreign currency travellers' cheque and the credit/ debit/ prepaid card holder is
one and the same person.
enriched_requirements.object: made by the traveller through debit cards/ credit cards/ prepaid cards for travel abroad (for private visit or for any other

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The sale of foreign currency notes and coins within the overall entitlement of
foreign exchange should be restricted to the limits prescribed by the Reserve
Bank from time to time for the country of visit of the traveller.
enriched_requirements.object: limits prescribed by the Reserve Bank from time to time for the country of visit of the traveller

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Sales against Reconversion of Indian Currency
Authorised Persons may convert into foreign currency, unspent Indian currency held
by non-residents at the time of their departure from India, provided a valid
Encashment Certificate is produced.
enriched_requirements.object: unspent Indian currency held by non-residents at the time of their departure from India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Note (1): Authorised Persons may convert at their discretion, unspent Indian currency up to Rs.10,000 in the possession of non-residents if, for bonafide reasons, the person is unable to produce an Encashment Certificate after ensuring that the departure is scheduled to take place within the following seven days.
enriched_requirements.object: unspent Indian currency up to Rs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Note (2): ADs Category - I, ADs Category - II and FFMCs may provide facility for reconversion of Indian Rupees to the extent of Rs.50,000/- to foreign tourists (not NRIs) against ATM Receipts based on the following documents.
enriched_requirements.object: of Indian Rupees to the extent of Rs

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Display of Money Changing Licence
An FFMC/ non-bank AD Category II should at each of its business place, display at
a prominent place in or near the public counter, a copy of the money changing
licence issued by Reserve Bank of India.
enriched_requirements.object: business place, display at a prominent place in or near the public counter, a copy of the money changing licence issued by Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Foreign Currency Balances
Note: Inter-branch transfer of foreign currencies should be accounted as stock transfer and not as sales.
enriched_requirements.object: transfer and not as sales

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) An FFMC/non-bank AD Category-II should keep balances in foreign
currencies at reasonable levels and avoid buildup of idle balances with a view to
speculating on currency movements.
enriched_requirements.object: currencies at reasonable levels and avoid buildup of idle balances with a view to speculating on currency movements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Franchisees should surrender foreign currency notes, coins and travellers'
cheques purchased only to their franchisers within seven working days.
enriched_requirements.object: coins and travellers' cheques purchased only to their franchisers within seven working days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) The transactions between authorised dealers and FFMCs should be settled
by way of account payee crossed cheques / demand drafts/electronic funds transfer
through banking channel.
enriched_requirements.object: of account payee crossed cheques / demand drafts/electronic funds transfer through banking channel

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Under no circumstances should settlement be made in
cash.
enriched_requirements.object: cash

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) FFMCs/non-bank ADs Category II may obtain their normal business
requirements of foreign currency notes from other FFMCs and Authorised Dealers
(ADs) in India, against payment in rupees made by way of account payee crossed
cheque / demand draft/electronic funds transfer through banking channel.
enriched_requirements.object: requirements of foreign currency notes from other FFMCs and Authorised Dealers (ADs) in India, against payment in rupees

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
15From
July 1, 2024, value of foreign currency notes sold by FFMCs / non-bank ADs
Category-II to the public for permitted purposes should not be less than 75% of the
value of foreign currency notes purchased from other FFMCs/ ADs, on a quarterly
basis.
enriched_requirements.object: of the value of foreign currency notes purchased from other FFMCs/ ADs, on a quarterly basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Data of such sale and purchase should be maintained and made available for
audit / inspection.
enriched_requirements.object: available for audit / inspection

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FFMCs/ADs selling foreign currency may also ascertain the 'sale
to public' requirement of the buying FFMCs/non-bank ADs Category II, by seeking
relevant data from such entities.
enriched_requirements.object: to public' requirement of the buying FFMCs/non-bank ADs Category II, by seeking relevant data from such entities

conditions:
Where FFMCs/ non-bank ADs Category II are unable to replenish their stock
in this manner
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Where FFMCs/ non-bank ADs Category II are unable to replenish their stock
in this manner, they may make an application to the Regional Office concerned of
the Reserve Bank through an AD Category-I for permission to import foreign
currency into India.
enriched_requirements.object: the Regional Office concerned of the Reserve Bank through an AD Category-I for permission to import foreign currency into India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The import should take place through the designated AD
Category-I through whom the application is made.
enriched_requirements.object: designated AD Category-I through whom the application is made

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Export / Disposal of surplus Foreign Currency Notes / Travellers'
Cheques
FFMCs/non-bank ADs Category II may export surplus foreign currency notes /
encashed travellers' cheques to an overseas bank through designated Authorised
Dealer Category - I in foreign exchange for realisation of their value through the
latter.
enriched_requirements.object: notes / encashed travellers' cheques to an overseas bank through designated Authorised Dealer Category - I in foreign

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FFMCs may also export surplus foreign currency to private money changers
abroad subject to the condition that either the realisable value is credited in advance
to the AD Category - I bank's nostro account or a guarantee is issued by an
international bank of repute covering the full value of the foreign currency notes /
coins to be exported.
enriched_requirements.object: currency to private money changers abroad subject to the condition that either the realisable value is credited in advance

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Write-off of foreign currency notes
16In the event of foreign currency notes purchased being found fake/forged
subsequently, FFMCs/ non-bank ADs Category II may write-off the same after
approval of their Top Management after exhausting all available options for recovery
of the amount.
enriched_requirements.object: approval of their Top Management after exhausting all available options for recovery of the amount

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Prior to modification, it read as follows:

In the event of foreign currency notes purchased being found fake/forged subsequently, FFMCs/ non-
bank ADs Category II may write- off up to USD 2000 per financial year after approval of their Top
Management after exhausting all available options for recovery of the amount.
enriched_requirements.object: USD 2000 per financial year after approval of their Top Management after exhausting all available options for recovery of the amount

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The request should be submitted through the APConnect application (Inserted vide A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: APConnect application (Inserted vide A

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) All registers and books should be kept up-to-date, cross-checked and
balances verified daily.
enriched_requirements.object: and balances verified daily

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Transactions not pertaining to money changing business of the FFMC/ non-
bank AD Category II should not be mixed up with money changing
transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: money changing transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In other words, the registers and books of account should show
clearly the trail of transactions pertaining to money changing business.
enriched_requirements.object: of transactions pertaining to money changing business

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iv) Separate registers should be maintained for each establishment, if the FFMC/
non-bank AD Category II maintains more than one place of business.
enriched_requirements.object: establishment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Opening of Nostro Account by Authorised Dealers Category-II
Authorised Dealers Category-II may open Nostro Accounts after getting one time
approval from the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank, subject to
following terms and conditions.
enriched_requirements.object: getting one time approval from the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: i) Only one Nostro account for each currency may be opened;

enriched_requirements.object: the settlement of remittances sent for permissible purposes and not for the settlement in respect of forex prepaid cards

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: iii) No idle balance shall be maintained in the said account;
enriched_requirements.object: said account

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
26Non-bank AD Category-II should submit application for opening of nostro accounts
through the APConnect application.
enriched_requirements.object: of nostro accounts through the APConnect application

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Submission of Balance Sheet and maintenance of NOF: All FFMCs/non-bank ADs Category-II shall submit their annual audited balance sheet to the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank along with a certificate from their statutory auditors regarding the NOF as on the date of the balance sheet, latest by October 31 of the year concerned.
enriched_requirements.object: balance sheet to the concerned Regional Office of the Reserve Bank along with a certificate from their statutory auditors

conditions: if there is any erosion in their NOF below the minimum level
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
As FFMCs/ non-bank ADs Category-II are expected to maintain the minimum NOF on an ongoing basis, if there is any erosion in their NOF below the minimum level, they are required to bring it to the notice of the Reserve Bank immediately
An FFMC/non-bank AD Category II shall submit to the Regional Office concerned of
the Reserve Bank, a copy of annual audited balance sheet along with a certificate
from the statutory auditors regarding the net worth as on the date of the balance
sheet by 31st October every year, and a certificate from the statutory auditors
regarding annual forex turnover during the financial year, by 30th April every year.
enriched_requirements.object: Office concerned of the Reserve Bank, a copy of annual audited balance sheet along with a certificate from the statutory audito

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Participation in the Currency Futures and Exchange traded Currency
Options Markets
FFMCs and ADs Category-II [which are not Regional Rural Banks (RRBs), Local
Area Banks (LABs), Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs) and Non-Banking Financial
Companies (NBFCs)], having a minimum net worth of Rs.5 crore, may participate in
the designated currency futures and currency options on exchanges recognised by
the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) as clients only for the purpose of
hedging their underlying foreign exchange exposures.
enriched_requirements.object: currency futures and currency options on exchanges recognised by the Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) as cl

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FFMCs and ADs Category-II
which are RRBs, LABs, UCBs and NBFCs, may be guided by the instructions issued
by the respective regulatory Departments of the Reserve Bank in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: instructions issued by the respective regulatory Departments of the Reserve Bank in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
24.28 For opening Foreign Exchange Counters/branches at the international
airports in India, AD Category-I banks/ ADs Category - II/ FFMCs should adhere to
following conditions.

(a) Foreign Exchange Counters/branches in the arrival halls in international
airports in India shall ideally be established after the Customs Desk (Green
Channel/Red Channel).
enriched_requirements.object: the Customs Desk (Green Channel/Red Channel)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
However, Foreign Exchange Counters may also be
established between the Immigration Desk and the Customs Desk in
international airports in India subject to the condition that these counters shall
only purchase foreign currency and sell Indian Rupees (INR) and
along with a detailed time bound plan for restoring the Net Owned Funds to the minimum required level."

28 Para 5 of Section II reinserted as Para 24 of Section V vide A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: and sell Indian Rupees (INR) and along with a detailed time bound plan for restoring the Net Owned Funds to the minimum

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
"Encashment Certificates" shall invariably be issued by the money changers to
the customers.
enriched_requirements.object: the money changers to the customers

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Foreign Exchange Counters/branches in the departure halls in international
airports in India shall be established before the Customs Desk or the
immigration desk, whichever comes first.
enriched_requirements.object: Customs Desk or the immigration desk, whichever comes first

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
29However, the Foreign Exchange
Counters established in the Duty-Free Area or Security Hold Area beyond the
Immigration or Customs desk shall allow residents (along with non-residents)
to exchange Indian Rupee notes.
enriched_requirements.object: non-residents) to exchange Indian Rupee notes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The limits of carrying Indian Rupees by
residents and non-residents shall be as per the para given below at (c) and
(d).
enriched_requirements.object: para given below at (c) and (d)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(i) Authorised Persons, which are regulated by the Department of Regulation,
Reserve Bank of India, shall be governed by the respective 'Know Your Customer'
directions as applicable to them.
enriched_requirements.object: respective 'Know Your Customer' directions as applicable to them

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(ii) Authorised Persons, which are not regulated by the Department of Regulation,
Reserve Bank of India, shall be governed by 'Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking
Financial Companies - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025'.
enriched_requirements.object: Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Know Your Customer) Directions, 2025'

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Prior to substitution it read as:
"However, to enable the non-residents to convert their unspent/ excess Indian Rupees above the limits laid down at para (c) and (d) below, Foreign Exchange Counters/branches at the departure halls in the international airports may be established in the Duty Free Area/SHA beyond the Immigration/Customs desk." 30 Substituted vide A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: Duty Free Area/SHA beyond the Immigration/Customs desk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(iii) Authorised Persons shall ensure compliance of directions, as applicable to them,
by their agents/ sub-agents/franchisees.
enriched_requirements.object: as applicable to them, by their agents/ sub-agents/franchisees

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The Reserve Bank may at any time vary or revoke any of the existing conditions of a money changer's licence or impose new conditions."
SECTION VIII
[Omitted]32
32 Section VIII omitted vide A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: or revoke any of the existing conditions of a money changer's licence or impose new conditions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Prior to omission, it read as follows:

"'Fit and proper' criteria for directors of FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II

(a) The Boards of FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II should undertake a process of due diligence to determine the suitability of the person for appointment / continuing to hold appointment as a director on the Board, based upon qualification, expertise, track record, integrity and other 'fit and proper' criteria.
enriched_requirements.object: due diligence to determine the suitability of the person for appointment / continuing to hold appointment as a director on the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: The Board of Directors should assess 'fit and proper' status by calling for information by way of self-declaration, verification reports from market, etc.
enriched_requirements.object: status by calling for information by way of self-declaration, verification reports from market, etc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II should obtain necessary information and declaration from the proposed / existing directors for the purpose as given in the Master direction on Reporting.
enriched_requirements.object: declaration from the proposed / existing directors for the purpose as given in the Master direction on Reporting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (b) The process of due diligence should be undertaken by the FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II at the time of appointment / renewal of appointment.
enriched_requirements.object: FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II at the time of appointment / renewal of appointment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (c) The Boards of the FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II should constitute Nomination Committees to scrutinise the declarations.
enriched_requirements.object: scrutinise the declarations

conditions: where considered necessary to the appropriate authority / persons
enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (d) Based on the information provided in the signed declaration, Nomination Committees should decide on the acceptance or otherwise and may make references, where considered necessary to the appropriate authority / persons, to ensure their compliance with the requirements indicated.

(e) FFMCs / non-bank ADs Category - II should obtain annually as on 31st March a simple declaration that the information already provided has not undergone change and where there is any change, requisite details are furnished by the directors forthwith.
enriched_requirements.object: 31st March a simple declaration that the information already provided has not undergone change and where there is any change, re

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (f) The provisions of Companies Act 2013 shall apply with regard to the age of the candidate.
enriched_requirements.object: the age of the candidate

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Further, the candidate should not be a Member of Parliament / Member of Legislative Assembly / Member of Legislative Council.
enriched_requirements.object: Parliament / Member of Legislative Assembly / Member of Legislative Council

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (g) Any change of directors during the year should be reported to the Regional Office concerned of the Foreign Exchange Department, Reserve Bank of India as given in the Master direction on Reporting.
enriched_requirements.object: Regional Office concerned of the Foreign Exchange Department, Reserve Bank of India as given in the Master direction on Reporting

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
All movement of Indian currency or foreign exchange should be effected by the
person(s) authorised, who should carry the supporting documents while moving
the cash.
enriched_requirements.object: person(s) authorised, who should carry the supporting documents while moving the cash

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The movement should be on the basis of requisition made by the
receiver and to the address of the destination.
enriched_requirements.object: of requisition made by the receiver and to the address of the destination

conditions:
If the cash is being moved from the office/branch of the Authorised Person
(AP)
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If the cash is being moved from the office/branch of the Authorised Person
(AP), it should leave the place only after it has been recorded in the books of
accounts of the AP.
enriched_requirements.object: after it has been recorded in the books of accounts of the AP

conditions:
if the destination point of movement of the currency is the
office/branch of the AP
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Similarly, if the destination point of movement of the currency is the
office/branch of the AP, it should be recorded in the books of accounts of the
AP, on the same day or on the date of receipt.
enriched_requirements.object: books of accounts of the AP, on the same day or on the date of receipt

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer of foreign currency between branches of the same AP should be
accounted as stock transfer and not as sale so that double counting is avoided.
enriched_requirements.object: transfer and not as sale so that double counting is avoided

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of doorstep forex service by FFMCs / Authorised Dealers Category II to
their regular customers, inter-alia, the processing and accounting of the transaction
should take place in the office of the AP and the transaction should be supported by
necessary documents for value received.
enriched_requirements.object: office of the AP and the transaction should be supported by necessary documents for value received

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The delivery of the forex should be done by
authorised officials of the AP only.
enriched_requirements.object: officials of the AP only

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
As far as possible movement of Indian Currency should be made through banking
channels (viz.
enriched_requirements.object: channels (viz

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The transactions
between authorised dealers and FFMCs should be settled by way of account payee
33 Inserted in terms of the Policy Decision, effective date February 22, 2017
crossed cheques / demand drafts/electronic funds transfer through banking channel
and in no circumstances the settlement of Indian Currency should be made in cash.
enriched_requirements.object: of account payee 33 Inserted in terms of the Policy Decision, effective date February 22, 2017 crossed cheques / demand drafts/elect

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The cash (INR) collected by the AP or its franchisee should be deposited to a bank
branch on the same day or next day.
enriched_requirements.object: bank branch on the same day or next day

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The cancellation of any move for transportation of cash should be properly
documented.
The movement of cash should be in sync with the documents.
enriched_requirements.object: the documents

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (DIR Series) Circular No.08 May 27, 2024 25 A.P.
